skill.name: nature-polishing
skill.description: Polish, restructure, or translate academic prose into Nature-leaning English using writing-strategy principles, curated Nature/Nature Communications article patterns, and phrase-level support from Academic Phrasebank. Use whenever the user asks to polish a manuscript paragraph, abstract, introduction, results, discussion, conclusion, title, methods section, or Chinese academic draft for publication-quality English. Also covers LaTeX layout/typesetting (排版) fixes — loose or sparse pages, stranded section headings, figures that don't fill the page or split across pages, "Float too large", multi-panel figure arrangement, and Supplementary Information that looks empty — via references/latex-layout.md. Also trigger on general academic/scientific writing requests even without the word "Nature", including academic writing, scientific writing, SCI/paper writing, English manuscript polishing, language editing, proofreading, and Chinese phrasings such as 学术写作、科研写作、论文润色、写paper、SCI写作、英文论文润色、语言润色、润色、改写、学术英语、英文写作.
skill.summary: Polish and translate academic prose into Nature-quality English with LaTeX layout guidance.
skill.author: Yuan1z skill, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only Markdown documentation files (SKILL.md, README.md, references/*.md, static/fragments/*.md) providing writing guidance for academic prose polishing. It has no executable code, scripts, network operations, or environment access. The 463 static analyzer findings are false positives: 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are Markdown backtick formatting in academic text examples; 'Path traversal sequence' flags are relative path references in instruction files; 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are occurrences of words like 'hash' or 'key' in writing context; 'System reconnaissance' flags are academic text analysis terminology. No actual security risk exists.

content.user_title: Polish academic prose into Nature-quality English
content.value_statement: Researchers struggle to make manuscripts meet Nature journal standards for concise, impactful scientific writing. This skill provides curated writing strategies, phrase patterns, and LaTeX layout guidance to transform drafts into publication-ready prose.
actual_capabilities: Polish manuscript paragraphs into concise Nature-style English prose
Restructure abstracts, introductions, results, and discussions following Nature conventions
Translate Chinese academic drafts to publication-quality English
Provide LaTeX layout and typesetting fixes for figures, floats, and page density
Apply Academic Phrasebank patterns for section-specific phrasing
Detect and correct common academic writing failure modes
limitations: Does not perform automated grammar checking like dedicated proofreading tools
Cannot access external databases or verify citations
LaTeX guidance is advisory; actual compilation requires user tools
Focused on Nature-family journal style; may need adjustment for other publishers

use_cases.title: Polish a research manuscript
use_cases.description: Rewrite a draft methods section to match Nature's concise, direct style while preserving technical accuracy.

use_cases.title: Translate Chinese academic text to English
use_cases.description: Convert a Chinese-language abstract into idiomatic English suitable for an international journal submission.
use_cases.target_user: Non-native English researchers in Chinese institutions

use_cases.title: Fix LaTeX layout issues
use_cases.description: Resolve stranded headings, oversized floats, and sparse pages using provided LaTeX layout strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Polish a paragraph
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish this paragraph into Nature-style English: [paste paragraph]

prompt_templates.title: Restructure an abstract
prompt_templates.prompt: Restructure this abstract following Nature conventions: context → gap → approach → key finding → implication: [paste abstract]

prompt_templates.title: Translate Chinese to English
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this Chinese academic text to publication-quality English suitable for Nature: [paste Chinese text]

prompt_templates.title: Fix LaTeX layout
prompt_templates.prompt: My LaTeX document has [describe issue: e.g., stranded section headings, figures splitting across pages]. Suggest layout fixes.

output_examples.input: We performed many experiments and found that the results show that our method is better.
output_examples.output: We compared our approach against three baselines across five benchmarks. Our method achieved a 12% improvement in accuracy while reducing inference time by half.

output_examples.input: 翻译这段中文：本文提出了一种新的深度学习模型。
output_examples.output: Here we introduce a deep learning model that addresses two key limitations of existing approaches: computational cost and generalization to out-of-distribution data.

output_examples.input: My figure keeps splitting across two pages.
output_examples.output: Use \begin{figure*}[t] for double-column figures and add [!t] to override float placement constraints. Consider using \afterpage{\clearpage} if the figure must appear before a specific text section.

best_practices: Specify the target section (abstract, intro, results) when requesting polishing for tailored guidance
Include context about the journal or audience for style calibration
Review phrase-level suggestions against your original meaning before accepting changes
Combine prose polishing with the LaTeX layout module when preparing final submissions
anti_patterns: Do not request polishing of entire papers in one prompt; work section by section for quality
Avoid using this skill for non-academic creative writing or marketing copy
Do not expect automated citation verification; manually verify all references

faq.question: What journal style does this skill target?
faq.answer: The skill is optimized for Nature and Nature Communications, with guidance adaptable to other high-impact scientific journals.

faq.question: Can it translate Chinese academic text?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes Chinese-to-English translation patterns designed for academic manuscripts.

faq.question: Does it check grammar automatically?
faq.answer: No, it provides style and structure guidance. For automated grammar checking, use a dedicated proofreading tool.

faq.question: Can it fix LaTeX layout problems?
faq.answer: Yes, the references/latex-layout.md module covers figure placement, float sizing, and page density fixes.

faq.question: Is it suitable for thesis writing?
faq.answer: It can help polish thesis sections, but the style is optimized for journal manuscripts rather than extended thesis prose.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: No, the skill operates entirely on local instructions and does not make network requests.
